title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
summary: Jack Roberts discusses Claude Fable 5 being unexpectedly removed and recommends running AI models locally using Ollama to maintain access independent of cloud provider actions. He demonstrates building a local NotebookLM alternative with open notebook and sets up model routing with OpenRouter across Claude, DeepSeek, and other providers.
writeup: Jack Roberts responds to the unexpected removal of Claude Fable 5 by arguing for local inference as a hedge against cloud provider actions. The video walks through running models locally with Ollama, building a local NotebookLM alternative using open notebook, and configuring model routing through OpenRouter across Claude, DeepSeek, and other providers. The takeaway: local and open-model setups keep access independent of any single hosted service, and routing spreads dependence across multiple providers.
